minimal_context: The student and teacher are meeting one-on-one between classes to discuss the found poetry writing task.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Today we are going to work on writing found poetry based on the literature.

STUDENT: I started my poem with 'The screams in the air are thick / I can feel her pain in my lungs.'

TUTOR: Okay, keep going with that.

STUDENT: I don't want to be caught off guard by what we are doing next in the reading.

TUTOR: We will just continue reading the text in class.

STUDENT: I need to ask for private meetings for frequent check-ins between classes to discuss the details of the day's lesson so I can avoid triggering.

options.A: The tutor should explicitly teach organizational strategies for the poetry task using a structured planning and organizational skills intervention.
options.B: The tutor should require each poetry assignment to undergo three revisions, grading only the final, third draft.
options.C: The tutor should never force the student to write about traumatic experiences, rather providing choice in writing topics.
options.D: The tutor should ensure praise statements occur more often than error correction statements during check-ins, maintaining a 3:1 to 10:1 ratio.
